name: Chicken and Lotus Seed Soup
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Blanch lotus seeds by briefly immersing in boiling water and then cooling quickly. Remove the reddish-brown tips from the lotus seeds. Wash and soak mushrooms in warm water until soft, about 30 minutes; remove stems and slice into thin matchsticks. Slice celery julienne lengthwise into 1-inch sections. Slice chicken breast and Smithfield ham (or other pungent pressed ham) into 1-inch strips.
Soup: Heat the stock in a 3-quart pan or sandy pot over medium heat. Add ginger, sherry, sugar, and salt. Bring to a gentle boil, then add lotus seeds, chicken, and mushrooms. Simmer for about 30 minutes until the lotus seeds are tender. Add celery and ham, and simmer for another 15 minutes. Mix the lotus root powder with a small amount of hot stock, then stir into the soup. Transfer to a heated serving bowl or serve directly in the sandy pot. Garnish with Chinese parsley before serving.
Note: Lotus seeds require about 30 minutes in simmering water to soften. Check them before adding ham and celery; if necessary, simmer longer to ensure they are fully cooked.
